\bottomhline
\multicolumn{9}{p{0.92\linewidth}}{\footnotesize $\Delta X_{\mathrm{CO_2}}$: plant-window minus background contrast of the B11 product ($\pm$1\,SE); $\Delta\mu$: same contrast of the applied correction. Bound: worst-case plume removal attributable to the spectral channel, from the background $\mu$-vs-$k_1$ slope and the plume-window $k_1$ contrast (ppm). Pct.: percentile of the plant-window variability-collapse ratio within the matched plume-free control windows; verdict: pass = consistent with the controls, flagged = the plant window collapses more than its controls. The two flagged windows carry an all-band $k_1$ signature and are resolved as cloud contamination, not plume removal (Fig.~F3; $\Delta X_{\mathrm{CO_2}}$/$\Delta\mu$ dashes: windows with no renderable band contrast). Channel attribution in Table~\ref{tab:nassar-attribution}.}\\
\endlastfoot
